commit.message:
fix: prevent path injection and open redirect vulnerabilities (#93)

## Summary
Fixes open redirect and path injection vulnerabilities (Fixes #87).

### Changes
- **Open redirect prevention**: Replace dynamic `req.baseUrl + req.path`
redirects in `autoLogin` middleware with hardcoded redirect targets
(`/terminal` or `/`) so no user-controlled data flows into
`res.redirect()`
- **Path traversal normalization**: Add `path.resolve(cwd)` in session
creation to normalize traversal sequences (`..`) before passing to
node-pty
- **Path injection (dismissed)**: The `fs.statSync(cwd)` validation and
`/api/dirs` endpoint are flagged by CodeQL as path injection, but these
are by-design functionality (TermBeam intentionally allows users to
choose working directories). These alerts have been dismissed as "won't
fix".

### Alerts addressed
- Alert #6: `js/server-side-unvalidated-url-redirection` (line 77) —
fixed
- Alert #7: `js/server-side-unvalidated-url-redirection` (line 89) —
fixed
- Alert #15: `js/path-injection` (line 134) — dismissed (by design)
- Alert #16: `js/path-injection` (line 281) — dismissed (by design)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(engine): safety FMEA test expansion (#147)

* test(engine): expand safety FMEA coverage with fault injection and state machine tests

Adds 113 tests covering:
- Fault injection: every fault type triggers correct state transition (9 types)
- Safety state machine: all valid transitions and invalid transition rejection
- Watchdog behavior: feed/timeout timing, arm/disarm, recovery
- Interlock verification: challenge-response, token mismatch, combo hold
- Torque limiting: clamping, emergency stop, NaN/Inf handling, safe-state entry
- Timing budgets: fault detection ≤10ms, response ≤50ms deadlines
- Multi-fault scenarios: cascading faults, sequential faults, fault log
- Recovery paths: fault → safe state → recovery → normal operation
- Fault injection system: scenarios, manual trigger/recovery, context updates
- Hands-off timeout, consent requirements, communication loss
- Proptest fuzzing for state machine properties

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: iFinder / iAssistant Conversation API integration (#1058)

## Summary

- **New `iassistant-conversation` adapter** — multi-turn RAG-based chat
via the iFinder Conversation API with streaming support, conversation
state management (server-side + client-side persistence), and automatic
session lifecycle handling
- **Citation system** — inline citation badges in chat messages with an
expandable citation panel showing document titles, snippets, relevance
scores, and direct links to iFinder document preview/download
- **Search status indicators** — real-time RAG retrieval status shown
during conversation (searching, reading, generating) with i18n support
- **iFinder document proxy** — secure endpoints for document preview,
download, and metadata access through the server (no direct
client-to-iFinder traffic)
- **Admin UI for iFinder/iAssistant config** — new configuration panel
on the Providers page for managing iFinder endpoint, authentication, and
JWT settings
- **Flexible JWT subject field** — configurable JWT subject resolution
supporting email, username, domain\username, and custom template
patterns
- **Auth middleware enhancements** — JWT, local, and NTLM auth
middleware now expose `username` on `req.user` for JWT subject
resolution
- **Three config migrations** (V013–V015) — auto-provisions the
iAssistant Conversation model, iFinder default config block, and JWT
subject field

### New files
- `server/adapters/iassistant-conversation.js` — provider adapter
- `server/services/integrations/ConversationApiService.js` — API client
- `server/services/integrations/ConversationStateManager.js` — state
management
- `server/routes/chat/conversationRoutes.js` — conversation endpoints
- `server/routes/integrations/ifinder.js` — document proxy routes
- `client/src/features/chat/components/CitationPanel.jsx` — citation UI
- `client/src/features/chat/components/SearchStatusIndicator.jsx` —
status indicators
- `client/src/features/admin/components/IFinderConfig.jsx` — admin
config panel
- `client/src/utils/citationTransformer.js` — citation data transformer

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify iAssistant Conversation model appears after migration runs
- [ ] Test multi-turn conversation with citation responses
- [ ] Verify citation panel opens/closes and displays document metadata
- [ ] Test document preview and download through iFinder proxy
- [ ] Verify search status indicators appear during RAG retrieval
- [ ] Test admin iFinder configuration panel (save, validate, test
connection)
- [ ] Verify JWT subject field templates work with different auth modes
- [ ] Test conversation state persistence across page reloads
- [ ] Verify existing iAssistant (non-conversation) provider still works
- [ ] Test with anonymous, local, NTLM, and OIDC auth modes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <223894421+github-code-quality[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use selectHostGroups for Zabbix 6.2+ host group API

Zabbix 6.2+ (tested on 7.4.7) renamed selectGroups → selectHostGroups
for host.get requests, and the response field changed from 'groups' to
'hostgroups'. Previously, inventory list showed no groups and
inventory apply always detected false 'groups changed' diffs.

Fix: use selectHostGroups in list_hosts() and normalise the response
by renaming 'hostgroups' → 'groups' so all callers remain version-agnostic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Box<T: ?Sized> blanket impls for all five traits

Enable embedded-io alloc feature so Box<dyn Trait<Error=E>> satisfies
ErrorType. Add ?Sized blanket impls for FileEnv, GitEnv, GitHubEnv,
NetworkEnv, and AiEnv so callers can hold dyn trait objects without
hardcoding a concrete error type.

commit.message:
feat: bypass Redis cache for non-mobile clients (okhttp UA check)

- songs List only reads/writes cache when User-Agent contains 'okhttp'
- fe-admin (node UA) always hits DB directly — no stale cache issues

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

uhttpd: increase CGI workers from 2 to 20, connections from 50 to 100

-n 2 allowed only 2 simultaneous CGI instances. With json-heartbeat.cgi
(and potentially other SSE streams) permanently holding slots, regular
CGI requests like pan/tilt motor commands had no available worker and
would queue indefinitely, appearing as 'pending' in the browser.

Increase -n from 2 to 20 to accommodate persistent SSE connections
(heartbeat, motor-stream, timegraph) plus concurrent API requests.
Increase -N from 50 to 100 to match the higher concurrency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: move client/server orchestration to network module, promote main_menu (#217)

* refactor: move client/server orchestration into network module

Move connection orchestration (sync barriers, state transitions, event
handling) from bin-level client.rs and shared/server.rs into the network
module as orchestrate.rs. NetworkPlugin is now generic over the app state
type so it can drive transitions without importing game-specific types.

- NetworkPlugin<S> takes in_game/disconnected state values
- ClientInputReceived moved from souls to network (pure network types)
- clear_net_id_index and DespawnOnExit moved to ThingsPlugin
- Directional light spawning moved to CameraPlugin
- Listen-server self-connect stays in client main.rs (needs AppConfig)
- Delete bins/client/src/client.rs and bins/shared/src/server.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: streamline WindowPlugin initialization using AppConfig

* refactor: promote main_menu to standalone module crate

Moves bins/client/src/main_menu/ to modules/main_menu/, decoupling it
from bins/shared. MainMenuPlugin is now generic over state (like
ThingsPlugin<S>), and a MainMenuConfig resource replaces direct
AppConfig access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update bins/shared/src/config.rs

commit.message:
test: harden plugin system with 120+ new tests across 3 crates (#151)

* test: harden plugin system with 120+ new tests across 3 crates

Add comprehensive test coverage for the plugin system:

- racing-wheel-plugins (plugin_lifecycle_tests.rs): 53 new tests covering
  lifecycle edge cases, PluginClass/PluginContext construction,
  manifest validation, quarantine/failure tracking, PluginError variants,
  version compatibility, registry/catalog operations, resource limits,
  native config presets, capability checker, runtime tracking, and ABI
  compatibility.

- openracing-wasm-runtime (wasm_sandbox_tests.rs): 46 new tests covering
  sandbox isolation, memory limits, fuel budgets, capability enforcement,
  guest-host communication boundaries, trap recovery, resource limits
  validation, and WasmError classification helpers.

- openracing-crypto (crypto_hardening_tests.rs): 42 new tests covering
  key generation, sign/verify round-trips, tampered data detection, key
  format parsing, invalid input handling, constant-time comparison, trust
  store operations, fail-closed mode, base64 encoding, SHA256 utilities,
  key fingerprints, detached signature lifecycle, hex key import, and
  system key protection.

All tests follow project conventions:
- No unwrap()/expect() - uses Result-returning tests
- Proper error handling throughout
- Follows existing test patterns in each crate

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: match CI stable rustfmt formatting in plugin tests

commit.message:
fix: security champion tooltip background not rendering (#729)

The tooltip on the security champion card (showing which components a
user is champion for) had a transparent background, making it unreadable.

Three issues were fixed:

1. Styling approach: The CustomTooltip used styled() with
   slotProps.tooltip.className, but MUI Tooltip renders its popup in a
   portal so the styles never reached the popup element. Rewrote to use
   slotProps.tooltip.sx which directly targets the popup.

2. Non-existent CSS variable: The background used var(--bui-bg-surface-1)
   which does not exist in the BUI theme, resolving to transparent.

3. Hardcoded border color: Replaced 'gray' with var(--bui-border-1) for
   proper dark mode support.

Now uses var(--bui-bg-app) for background and var(--bui-border-1) for
border, both theme-aware with proper light/dark mode values.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Håkon Frøland <31542827+hakonfro@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add defense-in-depth shell validation in SessionManager (#91)

## Summary
Adds shell validation directly in `SessionManager.create()` to prevent
code injection via user-provided shell paths. This provides
defense-in-depth beyond the existing validation in `routes.js`.

## Changes
- Import `detectShells` in `sessions.js`
- Validate the `shell` parameter against known shells before spawning

## Code Scanning Alerts Addressed
- **Alert #14** — `js/code-injection` (Critical) in
`src/sessions.js:125`

Fixes #85

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement AzureStorageProvider with Azure.Storage.Blobs SDK

Replace stub methods with real Azure Blob Storage operations:
- UploadFileAsync: uploads via BlobClient with overwrite support
- DownloadFileAsync: downloads via BlobClient.DownloadToAsync with directory creation
- DeleteFileAsync: uses BlobClient.DeleteIfExistsAsync
- FileExistsAsync: uses BlobClient.ExistsAsync
- ListFilesAsync: uses BlobContainerClient.GetBlobsAsync with prefix filter
- Graceful handling of empty/missing connection strings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(apple-silicon): fix macOS ARM64 build failure — half crate not linked (#3323)

## Problem

The macOS ARM64 CI build fails with:
```
error[E0432]: unresolved import `half`
  --> crates/bitnet-kernels/src/cpu/neon_mixed_precision.rs:14:5
```

## Root Cause

`bitnet-inference`'s `cpu` feature only enabled
`bitnet-kernels/cpu-optimized`
(SIMD targets: avx512, avx2, neon) but NOT `bitnet-kernels/cpu` (which
provides
`dep:half`). On aarch64, the `neon_mixed_precision` module requires
`half::f16`
but the crate wasn't linked.

Similarly, the root workspace `cpu` feature was missing
`bitnet-kernels/cpu`.

## Fixes

1. **Feature chain**: Add `bitnet-kernels/cpu` to both
`bitnet-inference/cpu` and
   root workspace `cpu` features
2. **Defense in depth**: Gate `neon_mixed_precision` module on `feature
= "cpu"`
   in addition to `target_arch = "aarch64"`
3. **Clippy**: Fix `needless_range_loop` in dense_forward attention
weights

## Testing

```bash
cargo check -p bitnet-inference --no-default-features --features cpu  # ✅
cargo clippy -p bitnet-kernels -p bitnet-inference --no-default-features --features cpu -- -D warnings  # ✅
```

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve DigitalOcean job progress tracking based on elapsed time

Calculate progress as elapsed time vs MaxRuntimeMinutes instead of
returning hardcoded 50% when running. Track job start times in a
dictionary, cap progress at 90% (100% only on completion), and fall
back to droplet created_at with 30min estimate when start time is
not tracked. Clean up tracking data on job completion/failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: FTS5 corruption + local model crash

Switch FTS5 from external-content to regular mode (rowid-based triggers).
The old value-matching 'delete' triggers were unreliable in modernc.org/sqlite,
causing all write operations (UPDATE, DELETE, tag add/remove) to fail with
SQLITE_CORRUPT_VTAB (267). Regular FTS5 uses simple DELETE FROM fts WHERE
rowid = old.rowid — no value matching needed.

Added schema versioning (schema_version table) with auto-migration from v1
(external-content) to v2 (regular FTS5). Migration drops old FTS + triggers,
recreates as regular FTS5, and repopulates from history_entries.

Fixed local model crash by capping NumThreads to min(4, NumCPU) per sherpa-onnx
docs (was runtime.NumCPU() which is too aggressive for large models). Fixed
Language parameter: mapped 'auto' to empty string since sherpa-onnx expects
ISO codes or '' for auto-detection.

Added debug breadcrumbs before/after each sherpa-onnx call for crash diagnosis.
Removed execWithFTSRepair (no longer needed with regular FTS5).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Admin.razor を更新

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct OIDC Trusted Publishing workflow for npm

- Restore registry-url in setup-node (required for OIDC discovery)
- Restore --provenance flag (triggers OIDC token exchange)
- No NODE_AUTH_TOKEN env var (forces OIDC auth path)
- Bump to v0.5.0 (skip ghost-published 0.3.x-0.4.x)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
